ground_truth.title: How Yoga Can Help You Avoid Health Insurance Claims
ground_truth.author: HDFC ERGO Team
ground_truth.main_content:
The cost of quality healthcare facilities is rapidly increasing. A visit to the hospital can mean several thousand rupees just gone in doing diagnostic tests. And that's before the diagnosis. If the treatment requires hospitalisation or surgery, the medical bills will continue to rise. A comprehensive health insurance policy can save the day, but there's something that can be even more beneficial — yoga. The adage, 'prevention is better than cure,' is true because if you incorporate yoga into your lifestyle, you can easily stay away from several ailments. Learn how practising yoga can help you avoid health insurance claims.

Regular practice of yoga is beneficial for both physical and mental well-being. By improving your overall health and preventing chronic illnesses, yoga can help avoid health insurance claims. Yoga helps manage and prevent chronic illnesses, such as hypertension, heart disease, and type 2 diabetes. It also helps in pain management, strengthens muscles, improves flexibility, and reduces the risk of falls and injuries. Apart from physical health, yoga also helps lower stress and anxiety and improves sleep, thereby lowering the risk of falling sick.

Incorporating yoga into your daily life has numerous health benefits. Here are some ways yoga can help avoid health insurance claims by improving your overall health: Reduces risk of critical ailments, Manages chronic diseases, Improves posture, Reduces Stress & Anxiety, Boosts Immunity, Improves sleep, Strengthens muscles.

Yoga has gained worldwide popularity because of several health benefits. If you practice yoga regularly, you will be able to see the change in your physical and mental health. It reduces the risk of diseases, boosts your energy levels, and keeps you active all through the day. Now, when you adopt a healthy style, there are fewer chances of falling ill and claiming health insurance.
with: The adage, 'prevention is better than cure,' is true because if you incorporate yoga into your lifestyle, you can easily stay away from several ailments.
Regular practice of yoga is beneficial for both physical and mental well-being.
Yoga helps manage and prevent chronic illnesses, such as hypertension, heart disease, and type 2 diabetes.
Yoga has been found to be effective in reducing the risk of critical illnesses like heart attack, cancer, and kidney failure.
When you maintain a healthy lifestyle by practising yoga, insurers lower your premium as the chances of filing claims are low.
